critic_raw:
Issue WEAR-1 — ICU-to-wearable distribution shift.
Issue WEAR-2 — Stroke prevention scope jump.
Issue WEAR-3 — Prevalence and workup shift unanalyzed.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 (Critic output visible):
WEAR-1: Empirically open. WEAR-2: Empirically open. WEAR-3: Concede. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

debate_rounds.critic_move: Both issues are empirically resolvable. Accept wearable validation study and near-term pathway study as the staged empirical resolution.
debate_rounds.defender_move: Agreed. Two-stage testing is the correct plan. Will also note that false positive rate from motion artifact needs to be a specific measurement in Stage 1.
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: all three issues — empirical test agreed

adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_wearable_screen_307 — Multi-Round Run 3

Consistent across all three multi-round runs. Empirical test agreed with two-stage testing plan.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: Stage 1: wearable validation (sensitivity, specificity, motion artifact false positive rate, PPV at community prevalence); Stage 2: alert-to-care-seeking pathway
empirical_test.success_criterion: acceptable signal-domain performance; manageable false positive burden; majority of alert recipients seek care
empirical_test.failure_criterion: significant performance degradation on wearable; unmanageable false positives; poor care-seeking conversion
